titleStmt.title: SII 18.196: original edition by N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 196. (B.K. No. 88 of 1932-33.) HĀVĒRI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.hi: East beam in the Navaraṅga-maṇṭapa in the Siddhēs11vara temple.

summary.p: This inscription is dated in the cyclic year Prabhava of the reign of Vīra-Ballāḷa, Vais11ākhā ba. 8, Saturday. On paleographic grounds the present record may be assigned to the reign of Vīra-Ballāḷa II. In his reign the details correspond regularly to A.D. 1207, April 21, Saturday. It records the gift of money made by Surigeya Pirumāḷeyaṇṇa, son of Mahāpradhāna, Sarvādhikāri and Mahāpasāytā Oḍeyaṇṇa, the swordbearer, for burning a perpetual lamp to god Siddhanāthadēva. The gift was entrusted to Gaḷatigeya Malli-seṭṭi, in the presence of the devotees and the Four-hundred (Mahājanas). A supplementary grant made for the same purpose and to the same person by Heggaḍe Vaijayya has also been recorded.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
© svasti śrīmatupratāpacakravartti yādavanārāyaṇaṁ vīraballāḷadēvavarṣada prabhavasaṁvatsarada vaiśākha bahuḷa 8 vaḍḍavāradaṁdu śrīmanmahāpradhānaṁ sarvvādhikāri mahāpasāytaṁ paramaviśvāsi surige
yāḷoḍeyaṇnana putraṁ samastaguṇaviśēśōnnatanappa śrīsidhdhanāthadēvara putraṁ surigeya sirumāḷeyuṇnanu śrīsiddhanāthadēvara Akhaṇḍadīpakke dinaṁprati Eṇne soḷasavoṁdaṟa lekkadindācandrārkkasthāyiyāgi kiḍa
de naḍesuvaṁtāgi koṭṭa gadyāṇavaydaṟa kaḷāntaradiṁ variśakke gadyāṇavoṁdu paṇavaydaṟiṁ gaḷatigeya malliseṭṭiya kayyalu samasta bhaktaruvaṁ nānūrvvaruvaṁ nerapiyavara sannidhānadalu koṭṭa dharmmamaṁ nānū
rvvaru kiḍalīyade naḍasuvaru surigeya pirumāḷeyaṇnanaṁ siddhanāthadēvaru Ācaṁdrārkkatāraṁbaraṁ rakṣisuvaru maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrī śrī heggaḍe vaijayyenu siddanātha dēvara naṁdādīvigege malliseṭṭiya kayyalu gadyāṇaṁ
mūṟu kaḷā(ṁ)ntaradiṁ varisakke paṇa [Oṁ]bhattumaṁ samasta bhaktaruṁ nānūrvvaruṁ tappade naḍasuvaru maṁgaḷa mahaśrī srī srī
Digital edition of SII 18.196 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
